category_title: Emotionally disturbed first-grader with trauma-induced behavioral challenges
minimal_context: Jamal is sitting at his desk in a class of 25 students and is asked to write his numbers on a piece of paper.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Jamal, please sit at your desk and write your numbers.

STUDENT: (Loudly, seeking attention from the rest of the class) I don't want to write numbers!

TUTOR: You need to practice writing them. Go ahead and start on your paper.

STUDENT: (Tears his paper)

TUTOR: Let's get a new piece of paper and try again.

STUDENT: (Falls into a heap and cries) My daddy was killed! (Kicks at his desk)

options.A: Provide specific times for purposeful attention through noncontingent reinforcement (NCR) by scheduling frequent, free access to peer attention throughout the day.
options.B: Implement a color-coded behavior chart to track the student's behavior across seven levels, ranging from 'Needing Parent Contact' to 'Super Principled'.
options.C: Use various pockets or holders to keep writing supplies and paper contained within the student's desk to maintain organization throughout the day.
options.D: Arrange seating assignments so the student is placed closer to the teacher's desk and next to a well-behaved peer throughout the day.
